chapter_title.ar: بَابُ صَيْدِ الْحَرَمِ وَ مَا تَجِبُ فِيهِ الْكَفَّارَةُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 21 – Hunt of the Al-Harram and what expiation is Obligated with regards to it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/86)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/86)

parts.text: زُرَارَةَ أَنَّ الْحَكَمَ سَأَلَ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام )

text: الْحَرَمِ مَقْصُوصَةٌ فَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) انْتِفْهَا وَ أَحْسِنْ إِلَيْهَا وَ اعْلِفْهَا حَتَّى إِذَا اسْتَوَى رِيشُهَا فَخَلِّ سَبِيلَهَا .

en.hubeali: Ali, from his father, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Hareyz, from Zurara that,

‘Al-Hakam asked Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> about a man who was gifted a clipped dove inside the Al-Harram. So Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Take it and be good to it, and look after it until when its feathers are complete (grown back), so free its way’.
en.sarwar: 5. Ali has narrated from his father from Hammad ibn ‘Isa form Hariz from Zurarah who has said the following: “Al-Hakam once asked abu Ja’far ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who receives a pigeon as a gift in al- Haram (the sacred area) with its wings shortened. He (the Imam) said, ‘He takes it, looks after it and feeds it until its wings grow, then sets it free.’”
